REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO SUBSTITUTE JOAN BLAND FOR ESTATE OF EDWARD C. BLAND DENIED. CCP 377.41 DOES NOT PROVIDE FOR THE RELIEF REQUESTED. PER CCP 377.41 A PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE OR A SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST MAY BE SUBSTITUTED IN PLACE OF A "DECEDENT" NOT IN PLACE OF "THE ESTATE OF [A DECEDENT]." FURTHER, UNDER CCP 377.20 A CAUSE OF ACTION ONLY SURVIVES THE DEATH OF "A PERSON WHO WOULD HAVE BEEN LIABLE." PLAINTIFF PROVIDES NO ARGUMENT OR AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT MR. BLAND, WHO DIED IN JUNE OF 2009, WOULD HAVE BEEN LIABLE FOR THE EVENTS OCCURRING ON OR AFTER AUGUST 1, 2012. PLAINTIFF FURTHER PROVIDES NO FACTS OR AUTHORITY THAT MR. BLAND'S INTEREST AT ISSUE IN THIS CASE WAS EVER IN HIS "ESTATE" (E.G. WAS NOT IN A TRUST) OR COULD HAVE PASSED TO A "SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST" WITHOUT A PROPER PROBATE PROCEEDING. THE COURT NOTES THAT ON FEBRUARY 11, 2019 PLAINTIFF'S EX-PARTE DOE AMENDMENT APPLICATION WAS DENIED WITH INSTRUCTIONS TO FILE A REGULARLY NOTICED MOTION FOR SUCH RELIEF, I.E. TO NAME A PROPER PARTY LIABLE FOR THE ASSERTED BREACHES/WRONGDOINGS OCCURRING ON OR AFTER AUGUST 1, 2012. NO SUCH MOTION WAS FILED.

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Sep-18-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michal Jacobs, Esq, 415-4454696, appearing for Poodles Inc., Plaintiff and opposing party.

Robert Niemann, Esq., 415-948-2827, appearing for Estate of Bland, for Defendant moving party.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT THE ESTATE OF EDWARD C. BLAND MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is GRANTED. Estate of Edward Bland is not a proper defendant. Estates cannot sue or be sued. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Substitute Joan Bland For Estate Of Edward C. Bland
Off Calendar Oct-04-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michal Jacobs, Esq, 415-4454696, appearing for Poodles Inc., Plaintiff and opposing party.

Robert Niemann, Esq., 415-948-2827, appearing for Estate of Bland, for Defendant moving party.

Argued. Mr. Jacobs stated that he contested to the tenative, that Joan Bland is the current owner of the company and provided case background regarding his actions in trying to file a noticed motion for a doe amendment. Mr. Niemann argued that there is no factual basis.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF POODLES, INC., PATRICK & FRIENDS, INC., CBD PROPERTIES, LLC, JILL WILLIAMSON Notice Of Motion And Motion To Substitute Joan Bland For Estate Of Edward C. Bland is DENIED. CCP 377.41 does not provide for the relief requested. Per CCP 377.41 a personal representative or a successor in interest may be substituted in place of a "decedent" not in place of "the estate of [a decedent]." Further, under CCP 377.20 a cause of action only survives the death of "a person who would have been liable." Plaintiff provides no argument or authority for the proposition that Mr. Bland, who died in June of 2009, would have been liable for the events occurring on or after August 1, 2012. Plaintiff further provides no facts or authority that Mr. Bland's interest at issue in this case was ever in his "estate" (e.g. was not in a trust) or could have passed to a "successor in interest" without a proper probate proceeding. The Court notes that on February 11, 2019 Plaintiff's ex-parte DOE amendment application was denied with instructions to file a regularly noticed motion for such relief, i.e. to name a proper party liable for the asserted breaches/wrongdoings occurring on or after August 1, 2012. No such motion was filed. Judge Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Good Faith Settlement Ccp Sections 877 And 877.6
Off Calendar Sep-13-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS POODLES, INC., PATRICK & FRIENDS, INC., CBD PROPERTIES, LLC, JILL WILLIAMSON'S MOTION FOR GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT CCP SECTIONS 877 AND 877.6

Appearances: Micah R. Jacobs of the Jacobs Law Group SF (415.445.4696) for plaintiffs and moving parties; Jamie C. Couche of Anderson & Poole (415.956.6413) appearing for defendants All Animals Properties LP and 1333 Ninth Ave LLC; Robert S. Niemann of Keller & Heckman (415.948.2827) for defendant Estate of Edward C. Bland; Norman Green, defendant appearing in propria persona with his power of attorney Randi Austell; Paul Perdue (415.291.0474) appearing for defendants.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DENIED to the extent the motion seeks either (1) the funding of the settlement via a distribution of profits to Dr. Green only; or (2) a distribution of profits to all 18 partners See Supplemental reply 3:23-24. Contrary to Plaintiff's argument this Court has no authority to order profit distribution in this case on a Motion for Good Faith Settlement. If parties wish to seek distribution of sale proceeds (See April 22, 2016 Stipulation and Order), such request shall be made properly. Fretz v. Burke (1967) 247 Cal.App.2d 743 is distinguishable and does not support Plaintiff's request under the circumstances of this case and this motion. Order signed. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Mary J. Visiglio, CSR 10391 (Veritext Legal Solutions, Tel: 866.299.5127) (501/REQ)

Notice Of Motion For Relief Under Ccp 473
Off Calendar Sep-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Paul Perdue, Esq. (415-291-0474) for Defendant/opposing party Roger Kuhn.
No other Appearance.

No Argument. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF POODLES, INC.'S Motion For Relief Under CCP 473. is DENIED. Plaintiff moves for relief of "vacating the judgment in Poodles I and allowing Plaintiff Poodles, Inc. to file an amended complaint in Poodles I, and/or to consolidate the two cases so that Plaintiff Poodles, Inc. can proceed with its claims on the merits." (Motion 1:13-15). Plaintiff provides no authority for the proposition that there are valid grounds to disturb a judgment in Poodles I, or that an action in which a judgment has been entered may be consolidated with another action. Plaintiff had its day in Court, and had a full opportunity to present its claims against the Defendant. Order is signed in open court. (see scanned order for details)
Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)
